text.title: Product Discovery Examples
text.summary: Two discovery examples — AI writing feature and market expansion — showing assumption tables, test methods, results, and go/no-go decisions.

example.note: 4 assumptions tested across value, feasibility, and business risk. Decision: proceed with MVP after full validation.
example.code:
Opportunity: AI-powered writing assistant
Assumptions tested: | Assumption | Risk Type | Test | Result | |------------|-----------|------|--------| | Users struggle with writing | Value | 10 customer interviews | Validated (8/10 confirmed problem) | | Users would use AI for help | Value | Fake door test (banner link) | 15% click-through (threshold: 10%) — validated | | We can build accurate AI | Feasibility | Technical spike, 2 days | Possible, requires 2 months integration work | | Users will pay $10/month | Business | Pricing survey (n=50) | $10/mo acceptable to 60% of respondents — validated |
Decision: Proceed to MVP, 4-week delivery target.

example.note: 4 assumptions tested. Decision: proceed with localization plan — assumptions validated but scope is larger than initial estimate.
example.code:
Opportunity: Launch product in Germany
Assumptions tested: | Assumption | Risk Type | Test | Result | |------------|-----------|------|--------| | Same product works without changes | Value | 5 DE customer interviews | Needs localization (strings, date formats, GDPR) | | Existing payment methods work | Feasibility | Technical research | Need SEPA + Klarna — 3 weeks extra | | Support team can handle German | Business | Team assessment | Need 1 German-speaking support hire | | Market size justifies effort | Business | Market research | €5M SAM in target segment — viable |
Decision: Proceed with localization plan, 8-week timeline (not 4 as originally assumed).
